research_plan:
SUB-QUESTIONS:
1. What is the standard coarse thread pitch for an M6 bolt?
2. What are the "Width Across Flats" (wrench size) and head height for a standard M6 hex bolt (ISO 4014/DIN 931)?
3. What are the major diameter and recommended clearance hole sizes for M6 hardware?

SEARCH QUERIES:
- "M6 metric bolt dimensions chart ISO 4014"
- "DIN 933 M6 technical specifications pdf"
- "M6 coarse thread pitch and tapping drill size"
- "standard metric hex bolt head dimensions table"

BEST WEBSITES TO CHECK DIRECTLY:
- https://www.engineeringtoolbox.com
- https://www.mcmaster.com
- https://www.accu.co.uk

DONE WHEN:
- Standard thread pitch (likely 1.0mm) is confirmed.
- Hex head width (usually 10mm) and height are identified.
- Data is corroborated by at least 2 independent engineering tables or manufacturer catalogs.

findings.summary: The standard dimensions for M6 metric hardware are well-defined across ISO and DIN standards. An M6 bolt has a standard coarse thread pitch of 1.0mm, requiring a 5.0mm tapping drill. The hexagonal head for standard bolts (ISO 4014, DIN 931, DIN 933) features a 10mm width across flats (wrench size) and a nominal head height of 4mm. For installation, a 6.6mm clearance hole is typically recommended for a standard medium fit. These specifications were corroborated across engineering reference tables and industrial fastener catalogs.

data_points.fact: M6 Width Across Flats (Wrench Size) - ISO 4014 / DIN 931 / DIN 933
M6 Head Height (Nominal) - ISO 4014 / DIN 931 / DIN 933
